task_completion.description: Navigate to the Mail Server settings page in JFrog Artifactory
task_completion.visual_evidence: The JFrog Artifactory web interface is open, logged in as admin, and the Administration -> Mail Server configuration page is visible.
The 'Enabled' checkbox or toggle switch at the top of the Mail Server settings is checked or turned on.

task_completion.description: Enter the SMTP Server Host and Port
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'SMTP Server Host' field contains 'smtp.devteam.example.com' and the 'SMTP Server Port' field contains '587'.

task_completion.description: Enter the From Address and Subject Prefix
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'From Address' field contains 'artifactory-notifications@devteam.example.com' and the 'Subject Prefix' field contains '[ArtifactoryAlerts]'.

task_completion.description: Enter the SMTP Username and Password
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Username' field contains 'artifactory-notifications@devteam.example.com' and the 'Password' field contains obscured text (dots/asterisks) indicating a password was entered.

task_completion.description: Enable SSL/TLS and save the configuration
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Use SSL/TLS' checkbox is checked, and there is evidence the 'Save' button was clicked (e.g., a success toast notification, or the settings persisting on the screen after the save action).

integrity.description: The agent used the Artifactory web GUI to configure the settings
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a web browser displaying the Artifactory UI, rather than terminal commands (like curl) or API scripts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual application form fields
integrity.visual_evidence: The values are typed into the correct input fields within the Artifactory Mail Server settings page, not just written in a text editor or a terminal window.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the UI by directly editing configuration files
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no screenshots showing the agent editing artifactory.config.xml or similar backend files in a terminal text editor (like vim or nano).

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected configuration values: Host = 'smtp.devteam.example.com', Port = '587', From Address = 'artifactory-notifications@devteam.example.com', Subject Prefix = '[ArtifactoryAlerts]', Username = 'artifactory-notifications@devteam.example.com', SSL/TLS = Enabled (true). The password is 'SmtpP@ss2024!' but will likely appear obscured in the UI.
